name: Sunflower Honey Wheat Bread (Bread Making in a Bag)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine in a 2-gallon heavy-duty freezer bag: 1 cup warm water, 2 packages active dry yeast, 1/3 cup honey, and 1 cup all-purpose flour.
2. Squeeze the upper part of the bag to force air out. Close the top of the bag tightly.
3. Rest the bag on a table. Mix with fingers for about 20 seconds or until all ingredients are completely blended.
4. Add the remaining ingredients (whole wheat flour and sunflower seeds or raisins) except for the flour and sunflower seeds/raisins.
5. Mix by working the bag with fingers. Gradually add 2 cups whole wheat flour and 1 cup sunflower seeds or raisins, whichever you prefer.
6. Gradually add the remaining all-purpose flour until a stiff dough is formed, or until the dough pulls away from the bag (reserve about half a cup of flour for the kneading process).
7. Turn the dough out on a lightly floured board, divided into thirds (or two and bake in two 9x5x3-inch pans).
8. Knead each part well until the dough is smooth and elastic. Add more flour only if needed to prevent sticking.
9. Cover with plastic mixing bag and let rise for 30 minutes or until doubled in bulk.
10. Shape the dough into loaves and place them in an 8-1/2x4-1/2x2-1/4 inch loaf pan (makes 3 loaves in this size pan).
11. Cover loosely with plastic bag and let rise in a warm place for 45 to 60 minutes or until doubled.
12. Uncover and bake on the lower rack of an oven set to 400°F for 30 to 35 minutes, or until golden brown (you may want to cover loosely with foil during the last 10 to 15 minutes to prevent the crust from becoming too brown).
13. Brush tops with melted butter; remove from pans and cool on racks.
